name: Otis Redding
abstract: Otis Ray Redding Jr. (September 9, 1941 – December 10, 1967) was an American singer and songwriter.

works_created.t: I've Been Loving You Too Long

about_quotes.speaker: David Spinozza
about_quotes.evidence: We were covering a lot of stuff: James Brown, Otis Redding, you know we played some Joe Tex.
about_quotes.doc_title: David Spinozza interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, NY, September 27, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Ellis Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: We had this band that we called The Directors. Everybody in the band... was supposed to back up Otis Redding on something, but I don't know what happened to that. It didn't happen.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ellis Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Buckingham,
about_quotes.evidence: Jackie Wilson, Otis Redding, occasionally Otis would have a band, they would augment it with some of us... we became like one of the key what, they called it, back-up bands.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Buckingham, interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, February 25, 2019 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Doug Carn
about_quotes.evidence: We played a little Wilson Pickett, “Midnight Hour,” a little Otis Redding. You know we played what the people wanted to hear.
about_quotes.doc_title: Doug Carn interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 24, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Theodore Leroy Bunn
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] It was great. Otis Redding and James Brown in the same show. [INTERVIEWEE] Uh-huh. Yeah, we played the Howard.

about_quotes.speaker: Carl Allen
about_quotes.evidence: And then later on, with the R&B stuff, you know, Otis Redding.
about_quotes.doc_title: Carl Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Atlanta, Georgia, January 4, 2013
